steps.title: Signal the concession
steps.content: The first sentence says Turner exaggerated when claiming to have invented jazz. The next sentence balances that criticism by saying his compositions and improvisation still helped shape jazz. The transition must mean "however" or "despite this."

steps.title: Reject result and restatement
steps.content: A signals a result, but Turner's contributions do not result from his exaggeration. B signals enumeration, which does not fit the contrast. C signals a restatement, but the second sentence is not restating the first. The answer is D.
